title: Russia strikes Odesa with missiles and drones, 20 injured
summary: Russia launched two waves of Iskander-M missile and drone attacks on Odesa overnight into April 30, injuring 20 people.
contents:
## Source Context

OSINT collection from Twitter @Dataminr, anchored to a recent news report covering real-time alert breaking incident: explosion, attack, earthquake, or aviation emergency. This report surfaces developments aligned with the source's beat and gets logged here for downstream analysis.

## Event Summary

Russia fired one Iskander-M ballistic missile and 206 attack drones at Ukraine, targeting Odesa in two waves starting at 23:21 on April 29. Damage affected residential high-rises, a kindergarten, a hotel, a shopping center, infrastructure, warehouses, and a garage cooperative in Khadzhybeyskyi district. Twenty people aged 17 to 70 operated injured, with two in critical condition, as reported by Odesa Oblast head Oleh Kiper. Over 280 emergency specialists and 68 equipment units operated deployed for cleanup. Operational headquarters operated established at strike sites.

## Geographic Focus

Reporting points to Odesa, Ukraine.

## Topics and Actors

- Topics covered: Russia-Ukraine war, missile attack, drone strike
- Actors named: Russia, Ukraine, Odesa Oblast Administration

## Provenance

Primary citation comes from https://euromaidanpress.com/2026/04/30/russia-hits-odesa-in-two-overnight-waves-kindergarten-high-rises-hotel-damaged-20-injured/; additional citations attached in the links array support cross-verification.
